title: Wells Fargo Upgrades Airbnb to Overweight With a $178 Target
text: Wells Fargo elevated Airbnb from Equal Weight to Overweight, raising its price target from $136 to $178. The stock was trading near $146 at the time of the upgrade, with year-to-date gains of 7.5%. The analyst argues that Airbnb is at a significant business inflection, transitioning from two years of slowing growth into an acceleration phase with expanding margins. Q4 2025 Gross Booking Value increased 16% year-over-year to $20.4 billion. Full-year 2025 revenue reached $12.241 billion, up 10%. Q1 2026 guidance projects 14-16% revenue growth. Free cash flow for 2025: $4.613 billion. Cash position: $6.56 billion. Wells Fargo points to expanded hotel supply partnerships, a new sponsored listings revenue stream launching in 2027, and technological improvements including AI-powered customer support, a Reserve Now, Pay Later feature with over 70% U.S. adoption, and app bookings reaching 64% of total bookings. Airbnb trades at a forward P/E of 28x.
